title: Cursor Positioning Mnemonics
text: Cursor Positioning Mnemonics Uniface uses mnemonics to position the cursor. Arguments Table 1. Mnemonics for Cursor Positioning Mnemonic Explanation cursor ( h , v ) Position the cursor. _cursor ( h , v ) Position the cursor in graphics mode. Parameters %h and %v —parameters in the output string for this mnemonic indicate the horizontal and vertical position where Uniface wants to position the cursor. Description A definition for cursor is required for display device tables and is not applicable for printers. Example: Examples The parameters h and v specify the coordinates of the home position, for example, cursor(1,1) or _cursor(6,1) . ^027[%v;%hH ^CURSOR(1,1) Copy For graphics mode, the coordinates of the position are computed, based on the [ w , d ] values of the current mode_ definition, that is, h times w and v times d . ’P[%h,%v]T’ ^_CURSOR(0,0) Copy Related Links Graphics Mode Mnemonics The graphics mode set and rest mnemonics in the graphics mode for displays that support them graphics mode. Parameters in the Output Sequence Some mnemonics include parameters in the output sequence that can be used to determine layout and formatting information, such as horizontal and vertical coordinates, numeric base zero (decimal, octal, or hexadecimal), zero padding, and so on. The output sequence can even include expressions that use the parameters incalculation, for example, to increment values. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
